ranges.label: Low Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity level is low, indicating that you generally think things through before acting and maintain good self-control in most situations. This reflects strong executive functioning and deliberate decision-making habits.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your thoughtful and planned approach to decisions and actions
Being cautious is positive, but also allow some flexibility in adapting to unexpected changes
Your self-regulation skills are a strength that supports good outcomes in work and relationships

ranges.label: Moderate Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity is within the normal range, suggesting a healthy balance between careful deliberation and spontaneous action. You are generally able to regulate impulses while remaining adaptable to changing circumstances.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your good self-regulation skills and balanced approach to decision-making
Give yourself extra time to think before making important financial or relationship decisions
Keep practicing focus and planning skills through structured daily routines and goal setting

ranges.label: Elevated Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity is somewhat elevated, meaning you may sometimes act or speak without fully considering the consequences. This pattern can occasionally lead to regrettable decisions or interpersonal misunderstandings.
suggestions: Try pausing for a few seconds before making decisions, especially under emotional pressure or stress
Use to-do lists, calendars, and schedules to enhance your planning and organizational abilities
Practice mindfulness meditation regularly to improve self-awareness and impulse recognition
If impulsive behavior causes recurring problems in work or relationships, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: High Impulsivity
ranges.description: Your impulsivity level is high, suggesting that you frequently act without adequate forethought. This pattern may be affecting multiple areas of your life including work performance, financial decisions, and interpersonal relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for a comprehensive evaluation of impulsive tendencies
Learn delayed gratification and self-control techniques such as the stop-think-act method
Establish structured daily routines and consistent lifestyle habits to reduce impulsive decision-making
Consider cognitive behavioral therapy, which has strong evidence for managing impulsive behavior patterns
Discuss with a professional whether impulsive behavior may be related to ADHD or other treatable conditions

attentional.label: Low
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is low. You demonstrate strong concentration abilities and can maintain focus on tasks without being easily distracted by irrelevant stimuli or wandering thoughts.

attentional.label: Moderate
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is at a moderate level. You may sometimes experience difficulty maintaining focus on tasks, particularly when they are complex, repetitive, or require sustained mental effort.

attentional.label: High
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is elevated. You may frequently have difficulty concentrating, find your mind wandering during important tasks, and struggle to complete activities requiring sustained attention.

attentional.label: Very High
attentional.description: Attentional impulsivity is very high, indicating significant difficulty with concentration and sustained attention. This level of attentional difficulty warrants further professional evaluation and support.

motor.label: Low
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is low. Your actions are generally well-considered and deliberate, indicating that you tend to think before acting and rarely engage in impulsive physical behaviors.

motor.label: Moderate
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is at a moderate level. You occasionally act without thinking things through completely, though these instances are infrequent and typically have limited negative consequences.

motor.label: High
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is elevated, meaning impulsive actions such as speaking out of turn, making hasty purchases, or acting on urges occur frequently and may cause interpersonal difficulties.

motor.label: Very High
motor.description: Motor impulsivity is very high. You frequently act without thinking, which may lead to significant problems in daily functioning. Professional guidance is recommended to develop better behavioral control.

nonplanning.label: Low
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is low. You demonstrate good planning and foresight abilities, regularly considering future consequences before making decisions and setting goals effectively.

nonplanning.label: Moderate
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is at a moderate level. You sometimes lack long-term planning and may occasionally make decisions without fully considering their future implications or consequences.

nonplanning.label: High
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is elevated. You rarely plan ahead for the future and tend to focus primarily on immediate gratification, which may affect financial stability and long-term goal achievement.

nonplanning.label: Very High
nonplanning.description: Non-planning impulsivity is very high. You almost never plan ahead or consider future consequences of your actions. Developing structured planning habits with professional guidance is strongly recommended.

ranges.label: No Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: Your body shape concern is within the normal range, indicating no significant body image distress. You appear to have a healthy relationship with your physical appearance and do not experience problematic preoccupation with body shape.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy and balanced body image by focusing on overall well-being
Appreciate your body for its functionality, health, and the activities it allows you to enjoy
Be a positive influence by promoting body acceptance and healthy attitudes in your social environment

ranges.label: Mild Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have mild body shape concerns and occasional dissatisfaction with your physical appearance. While these feelings are common, they may sometimes cause discomfort or lead to negative self-evaluation based on appearance.
suggestions: Be mindful of how social media, advertising, and cultural beauty standards may influence your body image
Cultivate a positive and compassionate attitude toward your body by focusing on what it can do
Choose clothing and physical activities that make you feel comfortable and confident in your body
Practice gratitude for your body health and unique characteristics rather than comparing with idealized images

ranges.label: Moderate Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have moderate body shape concerns and dissatisfaction that may be affecting your daily life, self-esteem, and social interactions. This level of body image distress warrants attention and proactive self-care strategies.
suggestions: Pay attention to your mental health related to body image and recognize when these concerns are excessive
Reduce frequent body checking behaviors such as weighing yourself repeatedly or scrutinizing your reflection
Consider discussing your body image concerns with a counselor or therapist who specializes in this area
Practice mindfulness and body acceptance exercises to develop a more compassionate relationship with your body
Limit exposure to social media content that promotes unrealistic body standards or triggers body comparison

ranges.label: Severe Body Shape Concern
ranges.description: You have severe body shape concerns and dissatisfaction that may be significantly affecting your quality of life, daily functioning, and emotional well-being. This level of distress is a recognized risk factor for eating disorders.
suggestions: Seeking professional psychological help from a therapist specializing in body image is strongly recommended
Body image distress at this level may be related to or develop into an eating disorder, so comprehensive evaluation is advisable
Avoid extreme dieting, compensatory exercise, or other potentially harmful behaviors aimed at changing your body shape
Seek supportive social environments and communities that promote body diversity and acceptance
Consider evidence-based treatments such as cognitive behavioral therapy specifically designed for body image concerns

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anxiety score is within the normal range, with no clinically significant anxiety symptoms detected. Your current level of nervousness and worry falls within healthy limits and does not suggest an anxiety disorder.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle with regular physical exercise and balanced nutrition
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing or meditation as part of your general wellness routine
Keep up regular social activities and connections to support your emotional well-being

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild anxiety symptoms that may be affecting your daily comfort and functioning. You may notice increased nervousness, physical tension, or worry that occasionally interferes with concentration or sleep.
suggestions: Learn and regularly practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing and progressive muscle relaxation
Maintain a consistent physical exercise routine, as regular activity is one of the most effective natural anxiety reducers
Reduce caffeine and stimulant intake, as these substances can significantly amplify anxiety symptoms
If symptoms persist for more than two weeks, consider scheduling a consultation with a healthcare professional

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate anxiety symptoms that may be significantly affecting your daily functioning, work performance, and interpersonal relationships. Both psychological and physical symptoms of anxiety are likely present.
suggestions: Consult a mental health professional such as a therapist or psychiatrist for a thorough evaluation and treatment plan
Cognitive behavioral therapy has strong research evidence as an effective treatment for anxiety disorders
Maintain a structured daily routine including consistent sleep, meal, and activity schedules for stability
Practice daily relaxation exercises and consider incorporating mindfulness meditation into your routine
Avoid self-medication with alcohol, sedatives, or other substances, as they often worsen anxiety over time

ranges.label: Severe Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe anxiety symptoms that are likely causing major impairment in your daily life and overall functioning. Immediate professional evaluation and treatment are strongly recommended at this severity level.
suggestions: Seek immediate consultation with a psychiatrist or mental health professional for urgent evaluation
A combination of medication and psychotherapy is often the most effective approach for severe anxiety
Ensure you have a reliable support system of family and friends who understand your situation
Learn to recognize the physical signs of panic attacks and practice grounding techniques to manage acute episodes

ranges.label: Low Body Appreciation
ranges.description: Your body appreciation and acceptance is low, suggesting a predominantly negative body image. You may struggle to recognize positive qualities of your body and frequently experience dissatisfaction with your physical appearance.
suggestions: Focus on what your body can do for you functionally rather than evaluating its appearance
Practice self-compassion and body gratitude by acknowledging one positive aspect of your body each day
Reduce exposure to appearance-focused social media content that promotes unrealistic beauty standards
If body image concerns significantly affect your life and well-being, consider seeking professional support from a therapist

ranges.label: Below Average Body Appreciation
ranges.description: Your body appreciation is below average, indicating some dissatisfaction with aspects of your physical appearance. While you may recognize some positive qualities, negative body perceptions tend to dominate your self-image.
suggestions: Try writing down one positive thing about your body each day to gradually shift your perspective toward appreciation
Choose physical activities and clothing that make your body feel comfortable, strong, and capable
Reduce comparisons with the appearances of others, recognizing that beauty comes in many diverse forms
Cultivate gratitude for the functions and health of your body by paying attention to what it does well for you
Consider exploring body image workshops or self-help resources that promote positive body image development

ranges.label: Moderate Body Appreciation
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of body appreciation and acceptance, with a generally positive attitude toward your physical self. You are able to acknowledge the value of your body despite recognizing some imperfections.
suggestions: Continue cultivating a positive and accepting attitude toward your body through regular self-affirmation
Maintain regular physical activity and enjoy the pleasure of movement rather than exercising solely for appearance
Practice intuitive eating by listening to hunger and fullness cues from your body rather than following restrictive diets
Celebrate the unique characteristics of your body and the diverse ways bodies can be beautiful and functional

ranges.label: High Body Appreciation
ranges.description: You have a high level of body appreciation and acceptance, reflecting a strongly positive body image. You respect, care for, and feel comfortable in your body, and you do not let media ideals define your self-worth.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive and appreciative attitude toward your body and physical self
Your strong body appreciation can serve as a positive example and inspiration for others around you
Continue caring for and respecting the needs of your body through balanced nutrition, activity, and rest
